event: Stage design for Richard Wagner's opera Lohengrin, Act 3, Bridal Chamber
symbols: arched doorways — passage and transition, evoking liminal sacred spaces
stone walls — permanence and solidity of an ancient dwelling
curtains — veiling and unveiling, separation between public and private
garlands and swags — festivity and celebration of union
Romanesque columns — weight of tradition and ecclesiastical architecture
description: A monochrome stage design rendering depicting a medieval bridal chamber interior. The scene shows heavy stone walls with Romanesque arched doorways, one revealing a darker passage beyond. Curtains drape from the left side. Decorative garlands and swags adorn the upper walls. A coat of arms or heraldic device appears on the left wall. The architectural space is rendered with careful attention to stonework texture and the interplay of light and shadow through the arches. The inscription at top reads the opera title and act designation.

_meta.title: Stage design for Richard Wagner's opera Lohengrin, Act 3, Bridal Chamber
